title: Code-to-Mermaid diagram generator for class, dependency, and module diagrams
purpose: Parses source files using regex to generate Mermaid diagrams representing class hierarchies, import dependencies, and module groupings.
module_context: Exports a schema definition for diagram generation parameters and a main handler function that orchestrates reading files and generating diagrams of specified types.

steps.content: Initialize an empty array to collect file contents.
Iterate through each file path provided in the input.
Attempt to read the file content as UTF-8 text.
IF the read operation succeeds, add the file path and content to the results array.
IF the read operation fails, silently skip the file and continue processing.
Return the collected file contents.
Initialize an empty array to collect class information.
Process each file in the input collection.
Match class or interface declarations using a regular expression.
Extract the class name, parent class, and implemented interfaces from the match.
Locate the class body by finding the opening brace after the declaration.
Extract method signatures from the class body using a regular expression.
Extract property declarations from the class body using a regular expression.
Limit the number of extracted methods and properties to ten each.
Add the collected class information to the results array.
Return the complete list of class information.
Extract class information from the input files.
IF no classes are found, return a default diagram indicating no classes.
Initialize an array to collect diagram lines.
Add the Mermaid class diagram directive to the lines.
For each class, add a class definition block with its properties and methods.
Add inheritance relationships for parent classes.
Add implementation relationships for interfaces.
Join all lines into a single Mermaid diagram string and return it.
Initialize an empty array to collect import information.
Process each file in the input collection.
Determine the relative path of the current file from the project root.
Match import statements in the file content using a regular expression.
Filter imports to only include relative paths starting with a dot.
Resolve the target path of each relative import to a path relative to the project root.
Add the import relationship to the results array.
Return the collected import relationships.
Replace all non-alphanumeric characters with underscores.
Trim leading and trailing underscores from the result.
IF the result is empty, generate a random ID with a prefix.
IF the result starts with a digit, prepend an 'f' to make it valid.
Return the sanitized ID.
Extract import relationships from the input files.
IF no imports are found, return a default diagram indicating no imports.
Initialize an array to collect diagram lines.
Add the Mermaid graph directive to the lines.
Track seen IDs to avoid duplicate node definitions.
Collect all unique file paths involved in imports.
Add node definitions for each unique file path.
Add edges representing import relationships between nodes.
Join all lines into a single Mermaid diagram string and return it.
Extract import relationships from the input files.
Group files by their directory relative to the project root.
IF no directories are found, return a default diagram indicating no modules.
Initialize an array to collect diagram lines.
Add the Mermaid graph directive to the lines.
Create subgraphs for each directory and add file nodes within them.
Add edges for cross-directory import relationships.
Join all lines into a single Mermaid diagram string and return it.

methods.name: handleDiagramFromCode
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, filePaths: string[], diagramType: 'class' | 'dependency' | 'module'): Promise<{ success: boolean; mermaidSource: string }>

steps.content: Read the files specified by the input paths.
IF no files could be read, throw an error indicating failure.
Generate the appropriate Mermaid diagram based on the specified diagram type.
IF the diagram type is 'class', generate a class diagram.
IF the diagram type is 'dependency', generate a dependency diagram.
IF the diagram type is 'module', generate a module diagram.
IF the diagram type is unknown, throw an error indicating invalid type.
Return the success status and generated Mermaid source.
